items.subject: Microsoft and OpenAI’s famed AGI agreement is dead
items.body: OpenAI and Microsoft's partnership-turned-situationship just got even less committed. And a clause about artificial general intelligence, which has for years dictated the future of their deal, has officially been dropped. On Monday morning, Microsoft announce…

items.subject: Choco automates food distribution with AI agents
items.body: How Choco used OpenAI APIs to streamline food distribution, boost productivity, and unlock growth—an in-depth customer story on real-world AI impact.

items.subject: An open-source spec for orchestration: Symphony
items.body: Learn how Symphony, an open-source spec for Codex orchestration, turns issue trackers into always-on agent systems—boosting engineering output and reducing context switching.

items.subject: OpenAI available at FedRAMP Moderate
items.body: OpenAI is available at FedRAMP Moderate authorization for ChatGPT Enterprise and the OpenAI API, enabling secure AI adoption for U. S. federal agencies.

items.subject: Microsoft and OpenAI end their exclusive and revenue-sharing deal
items.body: Microsoft and OpenAI are loosening their exclusive partnership, allowing OpenAI to offer its products across any cloud provider. Microsoft keeps non-exclusive rights to OpenAI technology through 2032, but will stop receiving a revenue share from OpenAI going forward, while still receiving capped payments through 2030 under existing agreements and remaining a major shareholder.

items.subject: The next phase of the Microsoft OpenAI partnership
items.body: OpenAI and Microsoft announce an amended agreement that simplifies the partnership, adds long-term clarity, and supports continued AI innovation at scale.

summary:
Here are four to six key points and one trend with insight:

**Key Points:**

* **16 new games will be available on the cloud in May, powered by NVIDIA GeForce RTX 5080 graphics**. This includes titles such as Middle-earth: Shadow of War and Cygnus, which are expected to offer high-quality graphics and seamless gameplay.
* **NVIDIA has released its Nemotron 3 Nano Omni model**, a multimodal AI agent that can perform tasks across multiple domains, including vision, speech, and language. This model has been released on Amazon SageMaker JumpStart and Together AI, and is expected to enhance AI-powered applications.
* **OpenAI has expanded its partnership with AWS to offer its GPT models, Codex, and Managed Agents**. This will enable enterprises to build secure AI in their AWS environments, further expanding the adoption of AI-powered applications.
* **NVIDIA's CEO, Jensen Huang, has been awarded the IEEE Medal of Honor** for his contributions to the field of technology, specifically for his work on AI and graphics processing.
* **OpenAI has ended its exclusive partnership with Microsoft**, which will allow the company to offer its products across any cloud provider. Microsoft will no longer receive a revenue share from OpenAI, but will retain non-exclusive rights to OpenAI technology through 2032.
* **The OpenAI API is now available at FedRAMP Moderate authorization**, enabling secure AI adoption for U.S. federal agencies.

**Trend:** **The shift towards cloud-based AI is accelerating**. With the release of the NVIDIA GeForce RTX 5080, the expansion of OpenAI's partnership with AWS, and the availability of Nemotron 3 Nano Omni on Amazon SageMaker JumpStart, it's clear that cloud-based AI is becoming increasingly prominent. This trend is driven by the need for scalability, flexibility, and security in AI-powered applications, and is expected to continue in the coming months.
